publicStories.name: Didier M.
publicStories.body:
I have to drive this road on a daily basis as the postie. I the last 3 months my mechanic and I have noticed premature wear and tear on the following: sway bar bushes, under body panels damage, cvboot wear, suspension definitely having a workout but holding so far.

Unfortunately Aus Post will not pay for this excessive wear and tear.

After contacting council and also notifying road inspectors with no success, with the road condition continuing it’s deterioration, I have decided to deliver once a week as I can’t afford to have my car off the road.

Friday 15 may will be the next delivery day, parcels arriving between now and then will be available for collection at your post office as per usual.

publicStories.name: Celine D.
publicStories.body: Damnages scared like hell on that road

publicStories.name: Layla T.
publicStories.body:
The conditions of the road effect my families health, safety and community connections - all factors which Lismore City Council hold in high regard for their residents.

As a resident of Maso Road I am required to drive on Fox Road multiple times a day. Each trip I factor in extra time to navigate the dangerous roads and now travel with a air compressor pump in case of tyre damage along with other safety gear - there have been multiple instances of needing to assist fellow neighbours who's cars have been damaged by road conditions. 

Along with road conditions effecting car travel, the road condition is also effecting walking and biking safety -  my children are no longer able to ride safely to their school bus stop. Apart from severe pot holes and damage, extreme levels of dust from the road make it impossible on a dry day to walk along the road without a face covering and have led to many neighbours needing to create physical barriers between the roads and houses with fences and vegetation. 

To add to all of this, we have recently been given the news that Australia Post is refusing to run a mail delivery to our roads because of the road condition. 

Both Fox and Maso Roads are severely damaged and degrade rapidly as soon as any cosmetic grading is done. Support by Lismore City Council to seal and improve these roads is imperative to ensure the health and safety of local community and people visiting the National Park.

publicStories.name: Gerard D.
publicStories.body:
The condition of Fox Road is a very sorry indictment on LCC. As other residents have noted, grading in recent years has been, at best perfunctionary. Undertaken virtually every time during wet weather with no attempts to deal with soft spots and potholes which just reappear in days. 
While working to clear my own driveway drain recently - blocked in large part due to the council drains not performing correctly - I watched in horror as vehicles are being forced to travel on the wrong side of the road around a blind corner in order to avoid vehicle damage! This is a tragedy waiting to happen and LCC is now clearly on notice that these dangerous road conditions are a definate public safety issue and a potentialy serious public liability issue. 
Fox Road is, and will always be a school bus route and the twice daily trips of the school bus (as well as other heavy vehicles) degrades the road surface very quickly and within days turns potholes into melonholes. Surely council has some established policy to properly maintain school bus routes to a safe and adeqaute standard?
As a ratepayer in the shire for many years, I would also point out that we get no garbage collection services, no water infrastructure, no sewarage services and frankly, very little else for our rates contribution. Even the Lismore tip has become ridiculously expensive and is now charging new levies for even recycleables. It really does leave us rural rate payers wondering exactly what it is we recieve for our ever increasing rates charges.
Council needs to act urgently on this and embark on a major upgrade of the road to bring it to an adequate safety standard including widening, corner reallignments, proper drain consrtruction and drainage, signage, and a staged sealing program begining at the most used section from Repentance Creek Road. 
Another perfuctionary grading and rolling will be woefully inadequate.

publicStories.name: Lydia K.
publicStories.body: Fox Road has always been a problem to drive on with all the potential holes and it can be quite dangerous with steep drop away and oncoming traffic. A proper investigation of the condition of the road leading to the potential of a better road surface, is required, especially as there are a lot of residents living along Fox and adjoining roads.

publicStories.name: Demetri C.
publicStories.body:
I am writing to raise serious concerns about the current condition of Fox Road, Rosebank, and to request urgent action to properly repair and maintain the road.
I live at 198 Fox Road, Rosebank NSW 2480, and access to my property is via Fox Road. Fox Road is a rural gravel/dirt road approximately 2 kilometres long. Lismore City Council is responsible for the maintenance and upkeep of the road, and I am a Lismore City Council ratepayer.
Fox Road was last repaired in December 2024. Prior to that work being carried out, the road was already in very poor condition, with numerous potholes and ruts. In December 2024, a motor grader and compacting roller were used to repair and resurface the road. Several truckloads of road base were also delivered and spread in sections where required.
Unfortunately, the repair work did not adequately address the underlying issue. The potholes themselves were not properly treated before the road was graded and rolled. In my view, the road should have been ripped with the rear tines of the grader to dig below the existing potholes, before being graded, rolled, and topped with road base where necessary.
Instead, the potholes were effectively graded over and filled in. As a result, the same potholes began to reappear within only a few weeks and have continued to worsen over time. I personally witnessed the grading work in December 2024 and can verify that no ripping was undertaken.
It is now May 2026, and the condition of Fox Road has again become extremely dangerous. Vehicles travelling along the road are forced to swerve to both sides to avoid deep potholes and prevent damage. This creates a serious safety risk, as oncoming vehicles are often approaching from the wrong side of the road while also trying to avoid the same hazards.
This situation is especially concerning because, twice each school day, the school bus must negotiate this dangerous road while transporting local children to and from school. This places children and other road users at unnecessary risk on a daily basis.
I am also the Deputy Captain of the NSW Rural Fire Service Rosebank Brigade. The Rosebank Brigade regularly conducts monthly training exercises in areas accessed via Fox Road. I can attest that the current condition of Fox Road would significantly hinder the Brigade’s ability to respond quickly and safely to a Triple Zero call-out in an emergency.
Fox Road is located in a high fire danger area, where rapid emergency response is critical to the protection of life and property. Any delay caused by the poor condition of the road could have serious consequences for residents, emergency service volunteers, and the broader community.
A serious accident or emergency response delay is waiting to happen unless proper action is taken.
I respectfully request that Lismore City Council urgently inspect Fox Road and arrange for proper remedial works to be undertaken. These works should address the underlying road defects, not simply grade over the existing potholes. A more durable repair is required to ensure the road is safe for residents, school children, bus drivers, emergency services, and all other road users.
I would appreciate a written response outlining what action Council intends to take and when Fox Road will be properly repaired.

publicStories.name: Mal F.
publicStories.body:
I have had numerous near misses on the corner known as Snapes corner. I have also recovered 3 vehicles on my section of Fox Rd using my tractor where they have moved a little too far towards the edge, got caught in the loose gravel and gone partially over the edge. There was one vehicle that actually went right off the edge onto the driveway below and was a right off. Luckily the passengers were able to exit the vehicle before it fell off the edge. 
The culvert drains are constantly blocking up, forcing the water to flow in the vehicle track lines. 
I personally do not want Fox road tarred as I believe this will increase the speed of vehicles and lead to more accidents and wildlife kills but something does need to be done about the continual failures in the same locations.

publicStories.name: Anthony G.
publicStories.body: Many near misses on the road, vehicle has suffered

publicStories.name: Billy J.
publicStories.body:
Fox Road, Rosebank has deteriorated into an unsafe and unacceptable condition.

Severe potholes now cover large sections of the road, forcing drivers to swerve into oncoming lanes to avoid damage. In places, the road edge has eroded away, with sharp drop-offs into bushland. Together, these hazards significantly increase the risk of collisions or vehicles being forced off the road.

This is no longer a minor maintenance issue — it is a clear and escalating safety risk for residents, families, school traffic, and service providers. The condition of the road is also impacting access to homes and local businesses, with deliveries and clients increasingly unwilling to travel along Fox Road.

Despite ongoing rates being paid, maintenance has not kept pace with deterioration. Temporary repairs are not lasting, and long gaps between works are making the situation worse.

We are calling on Lismore City Council to:

* Undertake urgent repairs to restore the road to a safe condition
* Provide a clear and transparent timeline for works
* Commit to a long-term solution, including sealing the road to prevent ongoing degradation

This is a basic safety and access issue. Without timely action, the risk to the community will continue to grow.

publicStories.name: Betty J.
publicStories.body: Every day I nearly have a collision with a car that is swerving to avoid the extensive potholes. Our cars have bottomed out so many times this past couple of months, about 5 flat tyres this year, tyre repairs and replacements. It honestly couldn’t get any worse.

publicStories.name: Jason B.
publicStories.body:
Several near misses 
Damage to vehicle 
Daily anxiety re access to our property

publicStories.name: Alicia H.
publicStories.body: Constant vehicle repairs which mechanic says are from the road condition. Almost daily near misses with other cars trying to avoid big or clusters of potholes

publicStories.name: Eric H.
publicStories.body: Riding motorcycle very dangerous with potholes and cars driving on the wrong side of the road. Have had to resort to patching potholes ourselves for to poor road maintenance. Guests have had numerous damages to vehicles.

publicStories.name: Thea D.
publicStories.body:
I have had 3 flat tires last year & had to pay $1000 to a mechanic to fix radial arms, steering & suspension. 
If I needed to be transported by an ambulance,  I would definitely fear the ride on the road, as it would be super bumpy & all over the road.
The community continues to purchase gravel to fill potholes & fix the road.
It's so dangerous navigating around the potholes, I feel like I may slip in the mud & swerve off the road or worse, into another car.

publicStories.name: Elise H.
publicStories.body: It damages my car as it grates the bottom every day I use it. Also getting unsafe, risk falling down high ravine while escaping the potholes, in two locations,

publicStories.name: Vanessa W.
publicStories.body: Sooo dangerous. Liability on my car, scary at times.

publicStories.name: Brett W.
publicStories.body:
Impact on daily life and several near misses, particularly when the road is wet and slippery and when the school bus is on the road. 

The community has also funded a delivery of our own road material and fix the worst potholes in between council grading.

publicStories.name: Ryan D.
publicStories.body:
My wife and i have had several near misses when  meeting cars on the road, its an accident waiting to happen. Fix it before someone gets hurt.
Recently replaced suspension on both our cars and now concerned about the cost of yet more repairs due to the road wrecking our cars.

publicStories.name: Gary W.
publicStories.body: Had verious car damage.shocks etc.

publicStories.name: julien P.
publicStories.body: Every day driving  over the large potholes has caused damage to my vehicles and trailers and is an accident waiting to happen

publicStories.name: Julian S.
publicStories.body: The road is in a constant state of disrepair and often opens up almost immediately after grading. It is dangerous and a large source of irritation. Previous structural work in some areas has improved those areas, yet there are at least half a dozen weak spots which cover the width of the road most of the time.
